gcp-architecture.title: GCP Cloud Architecture
gcp-architecture.description: Playrunner is designed to be easily deployable to Google Cloud Platform, providing a massively scalable, auto-scaling execution environment for your visual Playwright workflows.

local-dev/docker-images.title: Docker Images
local-dev/docker-images.description: Local development only. Both the Orchestrator and Playwright runner run as Docker images that must be built on your local machine before use.

local-dev/environment-variables.title: Environment Variables
local-dev/environment-variables.description: Local development only. Each service reads its configuration from environment variables. Defaults are designed to work out-of-the-box for local dev.

local-dev/firebase-and-data.title: Firebase & Data Storage
local-dev/firebase-and-data.description: Local development only. The app uses a live (remote) Firebase project even during local development — there is no local Firebase emulator configured.

local-dev/index.title: Overview
local-dev/index.description: This documentation covers local development only.

local-dev/services-and-ports.title: Services & Ports
local-dev/services-and-ports.description: Local development only. Each service runs on a fixed port and communicates over localhost or host.docker.internal.

local-dev/troubleshooting.title: Troubleshooting
local-dev/troubleshooting.description: Local development only. Common issues and how to resolve them.

local-dev/workflow-execution.title: Workflow Execution Flow
local-dev/workflow-execution.description: Local development only. This describes the full journey of a workflow run from the moment you click "Run" in the editor.

overview.title: Overview
overview.description: Playrunner is a visual workflow orchestration platform for running automated Playwright test pipelines. Connect triggers, integrations, and test runners by dragging nodes on a canvas — no glue code required.

tutorials/connect-github.title: Connect GitHub
tutorials/connect-github.description: Playrunner uses GitHub OAuth to clone your private test repositories. This tutorial shows you how to authenticate, select a repository, and set a target branch.

tutorials/create-your-first-workflow.title: Create Your First Workflow
tutorials/create-your-first-workflow.description: In this tutorial you'll build a simple two-node workflow in the Playrunner visual editor — a Schedule trigger that fires a Playwright test run.

tutorials/getting-started.title: Getting Started
tutorials/getting-started.description: This tutorial walks you through setting up the complete Playrunner local development environment from scratch. By the end you'll have the local stack running, the workspace configured through the setup UI, and the product app ready for login.

tutorials/index.title: Tutorials
tutorials/index.description: Welcome to the Playrunner tutorials. These step-by-step guides take you from zero to running your first automated Playwright workflow.

tutorials/run-your-first-test.title: Run Your First Test
tutorials/run-your-first-test.description: With a workflow saved and GitHub connected, you're ready to trigger a live test run and watch it execute in real time.

tutorials/understanding-reports.title: Understanding Test Reports
tutorials/understanding-reports.description: Every Playrunner test run generates a full Playwright HTML report stored with a unique run ID. This tutorial shows you how to navigate the report, find failing tests, and access screenshots and traces.
